_readme: Render recipe (starcomb resolution: CLI > this file > GENERIC). Tracks the generic pipeline; pin a knob here only with a measured, dataset-specific reason.

notes.spcc: narrowband mode on the composed SHO stack: R carries SII (671.6 nm), G Ha (656.28), B OIII (500.7); bandwidths at siril defaults (the corpus names the filter wheel but not the narrowband bandwidths)
notes.calib: prebuilt-master ingest: the corpus ships header-bare masters (calib/{dark,flat}_<token>.fits) — the filename token is the whole identity; per-filter darks pair with the per-filter exposures (Ha 600 s / S2 500 s / O3 400 s)
notes.capture: measured line balance: Ha-rich, O3/S2-starved; the ~501 nm O3 band sits in the skyglow so its sky rate runs high, and a digital palette equalization would multiply the starved lines' noise several-fold -- SNR parity needs much more O3/S2 integration than Ha
notes.OPEN_DEFECT: the central SPHERE (the target's defining feature) is missing from our renders. The sphere IS in the linear data and O3 carries it most strongly, but O3 runs several times below Ha in absolute flux, so a single LINKED stretch renders the scene as the Ha image: the sphere's hue sits near grain and the chroma coring neutralizes it. Root cause is architectural (no knob fixes it): the industry SHO chain stretches each line SEPARATELY before palette combination, while compose-then-one-linked-stretch cannot give a weak line its own lift. Caution: the linked-stretch dead-end was measured on calibrated BROADBAND (channel noise, no cast) -- its scope does not decide the narrowband-palette regime, and a per-line design must still answer the noise question that entry measured. Exonerated here: the VST chroma effect, separation leak, black point (linear-shift invariance), and the stars combine. A GHS finishing lift alone WORSENS the drowning (it amplifies the Ha flood around the sphere). Resolve the architecture, not a workaround.
